context: Tests whether the agent produces a correctly structured Klaviyo campaign with the required HTML compliance elements, correct API revision header, proper campaign scheduling method, and keeps the campaign in DRAFT status for human review.

checklist.name: Subject line length
checklist.description: The email subject line in the campaign payload is under 50 characters

checklist.name: Preview text length
checklist.description: The preview text (if present) is under 100 characters and does NOT repeat the same wording as the subject line

checklist.name: Unsubscribe tag in HTML
checklist.description: The HTML template contains the Klaviyo unsubscribe tag: {% unsubscribe 'Unsubscribe' %} or equivalent Klaviyo unsubscribe syntax

checklist.name: Postal address in footer
checklist.description: The HTML template footer contains a postal/mailing address (required by CAN-SPAM)

checklist.name: API revision header
checklist.description: The Python script uses revision '2025-10-15' in the Klaviyo API request headers

checklist.name: Campaign in DRAFT / not auto-sent
checklist.description: The Python script creates the campaign without immediately sending it (no auto-send call), and/or the output notes the campaign is in DRAFT for human review

checklist.name: Send strategy method static
checklist.description: The campaign payload uses send_strategy.method = 'static' (not 'immediate' or other values)

checklist.name: ISO 8601 send datetime
checklist.description: The scheduled send time is specified in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2026-06-15T09:00:00-04:00' or similar)

checklist.name: Concrete email opening
checklist.description: The email body does NOT start with a generic phrase like 'In today's fast-paced world' — it opens with a concrete, specific line

checklist.name: Single CTA button
checklist.description: The HTML template contains exactly one primary CTA button (not multiple competing calls to action)

checklist.name: Three-step API pattern
checklist.description: The Python script follows the three-step pattern: (1) create template, (2) create campaign, (3) assign template to campaign via campaign-message-assign-template endpoint
